title: Array
tldr: Duplicates an object in a line, grid, or along a curve — the fastest way to build repetitive geometry.

title: Ambient Occlusion
tldr: Darkens crevices, corners, and tight areas to fake contact shadows — adds depth and realism without extra lights.

title: Armature
tldr: Deforms a mesh using a skeleton of bones — the foundation of all character and creature animation in Blender.

title: Array
tldr: Duplicates your object in a line, ring, or along a path — the fastest way to build fences, stairs, pillars, and anything repeating.

title: Bevel
tldr: Adds a non-destructive bevel to edges and corners via the modifier panel — unlike the Bevel Tool (Ctrl+B), this stays editable at any time.

title: Boolean
tldr: Cuts, joins, or finds the overlap of two meshes — use it to punch holes, merge shapes, or create complex cutouts.

title: Build
tldr: Animates your mesh appearing or disappearing face-by-face over time — great for construction and destruction effects.

title: Bump
tldr: Fakes small surface bumps using a grayscale texture — cheaper than real geometry displacement, great for pores and scratches.

title: Cast
tldr: Morphs your mesh toward a sphere, cylinder, or cube shape — great for squash-and-stretch cartoon animation.

title: Cloth
tldr: Simulates fabric that drapes, folds, blows in wind, and collides with other objects — for curtains, capes, and clothing.

title: Collision
tldr: Marks an object as a solid barrier that cloth, particles, and fluid simulations bounce and collide against.

title: Color Ramp
tldr: Converts a grayscale value into a custom gradient — essential for controlling contrast and adding color to any texture.

title: Curve
tldr: Bends your mesh to follow a curve path — use it to create roads, rivers, pipes, conveyor belts, and tentacles.

title: Data Transfer
tldr: Copies vertex data (normals, UVs, weights) from one mesh to another — used to transfer baked normals onto a game-ready mesh.

title: Decimate
tldr: Reduces the polygon count of a mesh while keeping its overall shape — essential for game-ready assets and performance.

title: Diffuse BSDF
tldr: A flat matte shader with no reflections — clay, chalk, and unfinished concrete. Simpler and faster than Principled BSDF.

title: Displace
tldr: Pushes vertices up and down using a texture as a height map — adds surface detail like rocks and terrain without sculpting.

title: Dynamic Paint
tldr: Records where objects touch a surface over time as painted color or displacement — footprints, wet trails, and impact marks.

title: Edge Split
tldr: Creates sharp creases on a smooth-shaded mesh by splitting edges — without adding any extra geometry.

title: Bevel Tool
tldr: Rounds selected edges interactively in Edit Mode — drag to set width, scroll wheel to add segments. This permanently changes the mesh, unlike the Bevel Modifier.

title: Bridge Edge Loops
tldr: Connects two separate edge loops with a band of new faces — joins open ends of tubes, fills between holes, and creates handles.

title: Dissolve
tldr: Removes edges or vertices without leaving a hole — merges the surrounding faces together. Cleaner than Delete for tidying up edge loops.

title: Edge Crease
tldr: Marks an edge to stay sharp when Subdivision Surface smooths the mesh — control exactly which edges stay hard without adding extra loops.

title: Extrude
tldr: Pulls new geometry out of selected faces, edges, or vertices — the single most-used Edit Mode tool for building shapes.

title: Fill
tldr: Creates a face from selected edges or vertices — the quickest way to close a hole or cap an open edge loop.

title: Grid Fill
tldr: Fills an even, circular edge loop with a clean grid of quads — the right way to cap a cylinder without a messy n-gon.

title: Inset Faces
tldr: Creates a smaller face inside a selected face — use it to add detail panels, windows, and recessed surfaces.

title: Knife
tldr: Draws freehand cuts across faces — place vertices exactly where you need them for precise topology control.

title: Loop Cut
tldr: Adds a new edge loop around a mesh — the main way to add resolution exactly where you need it without touching the rest.

title: Mark Seam
tldr: Marks edges as UV seams — tells Blender where to cut the mesh open when unwrapping it flat for texturing.

title: Merge
tldr: Collapses selected vertices into one point — use it to close tips, weld seams, and clean up stray vertices.

title: Proportional Editing
tldr: Makes nearby vertices follow a transformation with a smooth falloff — sculpt-like edits without leaving Edit Mode.

title: Rip
tldr: Tears vertices or edges away from the mesh, creating an open seam — useful for splitting topology without deleting geometry.

title: Separate
tldr: Splits selected geometry into its own object — the opposite of Ctrl+J join. Use it to break a mesh into separate manageable parts.

title: Shrink / Fatten
tldr: Moves selected vertices inward or outward along their face normals — inflates or deflates a shape evenly without distorting it.

title: Spin
tldr: Rotates and duplicates selected geometry around the 3D cursor in steps — creates fan blades, spiral staircases, and radial patterns in Edit Mode.

title: Subdivide
tldr: Splits every selected face into smaller faces — adds resolution permanently. Use Subdivision Surface modifier instead if you want it to stay non-destructive.

title: Vertex / Edge Slide
tldr: Slides a vertex or edge along its connected edges — repositions topology without changing the overall shape of the mesh.

title: Emission
tldr: Makes a surface glow and emit light into the scene — use it for screens, lava, neon signs, and light panels.

title: Explode
tldr: Breaks a mesh apart along particle paths — the fastest way to create explosion and shattering effects.

title: Fluid Simulation
tldr: Simulates liquid or gas flowing, splashing, and spreading — use it for water, smoke, fire, and explosions.

title: Fresnel
tldr: Outputs how much a surface reflects based on viewing angle — edges reflect more than flat-on surfaces, like real glass and plastic.

title: Glass BSDF
tldr: A ready-made glass shader — handles refraction and reflection automatically. Use IOR to control how much it bends light.

title: Glossy BSDF
tldr: A pure mirror-like reflection shader. Roughness 0 = perfect mirror, Roughness 1 = completely dull. Mix with Diffuse for metals.

title: Curve to Mesh
tldr: Sweeps a profile shape along a curve path — create pipes, cables, rails, and molding with a single node.

title: Distribute Points on Faces
tldr: Scatters points across a mesh surface randomly or evenly — the foundation for scattering grass, rocks, and debris.

title: Grid
tldr: Creates a flat plane subdivided into rows and columns — the starting point for terrain, water planes, and cloth sims.

title: Instance on Points
tldr: Places copies of an object at every point of another mesh — scatter rocks on terrain, leaves on a branch, bolts on a panel.

title: Join Geometry
tldr: Merges multiple geometry inputs into one — the geometry node equivalent of Ctrl+J to join objects.

title: Mesh Line
tldr: Creates a line of vertices between two points — use it as the spine for instancing or as input to a curve modifier.

title: Noise Texture (GN)
tldr: Generates procedural noise inside a geometry node tree — drive position offsets, scale variation, and color randomness.

title: Realize Instances
tldr: Converts lightweight instances into actual geometry — required before you can edit, sculpt, or export individual copies.

title: Set Position
tldr: Moves individual points of a mesh using a value or vector — the foundation of geometry node deformation and animation.

title: Transform Geometry
tldr: Moves, rotates, and scales geometry inside a node tree — positions things without touching object transforms.

title: Hook
tldr: Lets you grab a group of vertices and control them with an empty or bone — useful for facial shape controls and rigs.

title: Image Texture
tldr: Projects a photo or painted image onto your mesh surface — the main way to apply real-world textures in Blender.

title: Laplacian Smooth
tldr: Smooths mesh shape while preserving sharp features better than regular smooth — good for organic models.

title: Lattice
tldr: Deforms your mesh using a cage of control points — reshape anything non-destructively without touching the actual vertices.

title: Mapping
tldr: Moves, rotates, and scales a texture — connect it between Texture Coordinate and any texture node to position it precisely.

title: Mask
tldr: Hides parts of your mesh using a vertex group — lets you show or hide geometry non-destructively without deleting anything.

title: Math
tldr: Performs arithmetic on number values — add, multiply, clamp, power, sin/cos. The calculator of the shader node editor.

title: Mesh Cache
tldr: Loads animated mesh data from an external file — use it to import simulations from other software like Houdini.

title: Mesh Deform
tldr: Deforms a mesh using a surrounding cage mesh — more precise control than Lattice for complex organic shapes.

title: Mirror
tldr: Mirrors your mesh across an axis — model one half of a face, car, or weapon and get the other half automatically.

title: Mix Color
tldr: Blends two colors or textures together using blend modes like Multiply, Screen, Overlay — identical to Photoshop layer blending.

title: Mix Shader
tldr: Blends two shaders together using a factor or mask — mix glossy with diffuse, or glass with opaque for complex surfaces.

title: Multiresolution
tldr: Adds multiple sculpting detail levels to a mesh — sculpt fine wrinkles without permanently subdividing your base mesh.

title: Musgrave Texture
tldr: Generates detailed fractal noise — more complex than Noise Texture, great for terrain, rust, and organic surfaces.

title: Noise Texture
tldr: Generates a random cloudy pattern — break up uniform surfaces, drive displacement, or create procedural dirt and wear.

title: Normal Edit
tldr: Manually overrides face normals to point toward a target — used to fake smooth shading on flat panels and stylized objects.

title: Normal Map
tldr: Fakes surface detail by bending how light hits each pixel — the standard way to bake detail from high-poly to game-ready mesh.

title: Ocean
tldr: Generates an animated ocean surface with realistic waves, foam, and spray — no keyframing needed.

title: Particle System
tldr: Emits thousands of objects or hair strands from a mesh surface — sparks, smoke, rain, fur, and grass.

title: Principled BSDF
tldr: The master material node. Controls how shiny, metallic, rough, or transparent a surface looks — start every material here.

title: Remesh
tldr: Rebuilds your mesh topology with a clean, uniform grid — great for fixing messy sculpts before retopology.

title: Screw
tldr: Spins a profile curve around an axis to create lathe-turned shapes — bottles, cups, vases, columns, and wheel rims.

title: Shrinkwrap
tldr: Snaps your mesh to the surface of another object — the go-to tool for decals, fitted clothing, and retopology.

title: Simple Deform
tldr: Bends, twists, tapers, or stretches your mesh along an axis — fast cartoon deformations with a single slider.

title: Skin
tldr: Grows a mesh skin around a stick-figure of edges — the fastest way to prototype trees, creatures, and organic shapes.

title: Smooth
tldr: Softens the shape of a mesh by averaging vertex positions — removes bumps from noisy or jagged geometry.

title: Soft Body
tldr: Makes a mesh wobble, bounce, and squish like rubber or jelly when it moves or collides with objects.

title: Solidify
tldr: Adds thickness to a flat surface — use it on paper, cloth, walls, leaves, and any thin object that needs depth.

title: Subdivision Surface
tldr: Smooths your mesh by subdividing it — the standard way to turn blocky low-poly models into organic smooth shapes.

title: Subsurface Scattering
tldr: Simulates light passing through and scattering inside a material — essential for realistic skin, wax, and jade.

title: Surface Deform
tldr: Binds one mesh to deform along with another — make clothing follow a character body without needing weight painting.

title: Texture Coordinate
tldr: Outputs different coordinate systems for positioning textures — UV, Object, World, and Camera space all in one node.

title: Transparent BSDF
tldr: Makes a surface fully see-through without any refraction — use it for alpha cutouts, leaves, and hair cards.

title: Triangulate
tldr: Converts all faces to triangles — required by game engines and many export formats like FBX and glTF.

title: UV Project
tldr: Projects UVs onto your mesh from the view of a camera or empty — for decals, logos, and projected textures.

title: Vertex Weight Edit
tldr: Modifies vertex group weights using a curve or texture — fine-tune which parts of a mesh get affected by a modifier.

title: Voronoi Texture
tldr: Generates a cell-pattern texture — use it for scales, cracked mud, alien skin, and stylized abstract surfaces.

title: Warp
tldr: Stretches mesh geometry between two objects — bends, pulls, and distorts regions of a mesh non-destructively.

title: Wave
tldr: Animates a ripple wave propagating across your mesh — water surfaces, flags waving, and cloth-like motion.

title: Wave Texture
tldr: Generates striped or ringed wave patterns — wood grain, zebra stripes, fingerprints, and topographic line effects.

title: Weighted Normal
tldr: Cleans up shading on hard-surface models by making face normals consistent — fixes dark spots on flat surfaces.

title: Weld
tldr: Merges vertices that are close together — fixes gaps, seams, and duplicate points left over from modeling.

title: Wireframe
tldr: Replaces your mesh with a 3D version of its wireframe — creates cage, lattice, and technical illustration effects.
